kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the KMS key that is used for encryption.

encryptionType.description: The type of encryption. Set to KMS_BASED_ENCRYPTION to use a KMS key that you own and manage. Set to FLEETWISE_DEFAULT_ENCRYPTION to use an Amazon Web Services managed key that is owned by the Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise service account.

errorMessage.description: The error message that describes why encryption settings couldn't be configured, if applicable.

creationTime.description: The time when encryption was configured in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time when encryption was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

cloudWatchLogDelivery.description: Returns information about log delivery to Amazon CloudWatch Logs.

customerAccountId.description: The unique ID of the Amazon Web Services account, provided at account creation.

accountStatus.description:
The status of registering your account and resources. The status can be one of:

  • REGISTRATION_SUCCESS - The Amazon Web Services resource is successfully registered.

  • REGISTRATION_PENDING - Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise is processing the registration request. This process takes approximately five minutes to complete.

  • REGISTRATION_FAILURE - Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise can't register the AWS resource. Try again later.

timestreamRegistrationResponse.description: Information about the registered Amazon Timestream resources or errors, if any.

iamRegistrationResponse.description: Information about the registered IAM resources or errors, if any.

creationTime.description: The time the account was registered, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time this registration was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

campaignSummaries.description: A summary of information about each campaign.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

summaries.description: A list of information about each decoder manifest.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

fleetSummaries.description: A list of information for each fleet.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

summaries.description: A list of information about vehicle models.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

summaries.description: A list of information about each signal catalog.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

summaries.description: A list of information about each state template.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

vehicleSummaries.description: A list of vehicles and information about them.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the campaign.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of a signal catalog.

targetArn.description: The ARN of the vehicle or the fleet targeted by the campaign.

status.description: The state of the campaign. The status can be one of: CREATING, WAITING_FOR_APPROVAL, RUNNING, and SUSPENDED.

startTime.description: The time, in milliseconds, to deliver a campaign after it was approved.

expiryTime.description: The time the campaign expires, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time). Vehicle data won't be collected after the campaign expires.

postTriggerCollectionDuration.description: How long (in seconds) to collect raw data after a triggering event initiates the collection.

diagnosticsMode.description: Option for a vehicle to send diagnostic trouble codes to Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise.

spoolingMode.description: Whether to store collected data after a vehicle lost a connection with the cloud. After a connection is re-established, the data is automatically forwarded to Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise.

compression.description: Whether to compress signals before transmitting data to Amazon Web Services IoT FleetWise. If OFF is specified, the signals aren't compressed. If it's not specified, SNAPPY is used.

priority.description: A number indicating the priority of one campaign over another campaign for a certain vehicle or fleet. A campaign with the lowest value is deployed to vehicles before any other campaigns.

signalsToCollect.description: Information about a list of signals to collect data on.

collectionScheme.description: Information about the data collection scheme associated with the campaign.

dataExtraDimensions.description: A list of vehicle attributes associated with the campaign.

creationTime.description: The time the campaign was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the campaign was modified.

dataDestinationConfigs.description:
The destination where the campaign sends data. You can send data to an MQTT topic, or store it in Amazon S3 or Amazon Timestream.

MQTT is the publish/subscribe messaging protocol used by Amazon Web Services IoT to communicate with your devices.

Amazon S3 optimizes the cost of data storage and provides additional mechanisms to use vehicle data, such as data lakes, centralized data storage, data processing pipelines, and analytics.

You can use Amazon Timestream to access and analyze time series data, and Timestream to query vehicle data so that you can identify trends and patterns.

dataPartitions.description: The data partitions associated with the signals collected from the vehicle.

signalsToFetch.description: Information about a list of signals to fetch data from.

name.description: The name of the decoder manifest.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the decoder manifest.

description.description: A brief description of the decoder manifest.

modelManifestArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the decoder manifest.

status.description: The state of the decoder manifest. If the status is ACTIVE, the decoder manifest can't be edited. If the status is marked DRAFT, you can edit the decoder manifest.

creationTime.description: The time the decoder manifest was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the decoder manifest was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

message.description: The detailed message for the decoder manifest. When a decoder manifest is in an INVALID status, the message contains detailed reason and help information.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the fleet.

description.description: A brief description of the fleet.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of a signal catalog associated with the fleet.

creationTime.description: The time the fleet was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the fleet was last updated, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

name.description: The name of the vehicle model.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the vehicle model.

description.description: A brief description of the vehicle model.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of the signal catalog associated with the vehicle model.

status.description: The state of the vehicle model. If the status is ACTIVE, the vehicle model can't be edited. You can edit the vehicle model if the status is marked DRAFT.

creationTime.description: The time the vehicle model was created, in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the vehicle model was modified.

name.description: The name of the signal catalog.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the signal catalog.

description.description: A brief description of the signal catalog.

nodeCounts.description: The total number of network nodes specified in a signal catalog.

creationTime.description: The time the signal catalog was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The last time the signal catalog was modified.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the state template.

description.description: A brief description of the state template.

signalCatalogArn.description: The ARN of the signal catalog associated with the state template.

stateTemplateProperties.description: A list of signals from which data is collected. The state template properties contain the fully qualified names of the signals.

dataExtraDimensions.description:
A list of vehicle attributes associated with the payload published on the state template's MQTT topic.

Default: An empty array

metadataExtraDimensions.description:
A list of vehicle attributes to associate with user properties of the messages published on the state template's MQTT topic.

Default: An empty array

creationTime.description: The time the state template was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the state template was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

id.description: The unique ID of the state template.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the vehicle to retrieve information about.

modelManifestArn.description: The ARN of a vehicle model (model manifest) associated with the vehicle.

decoderManifestArn.description: The ARN of a decoder manifest associated with the vehicle.

attributes.description:
Static information about a vehicle in a key-value pair. For example:

"engineType" : "1.3 L R2"

creationTime.description: The time the vehicle was created in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

lastModificationTime.description: The time the vehicle was last updated in seconds since epoch (January 1, 1970 at midnight UTC time).

campaigns.description: Lists information about the state of the vehicle with deployed campaigns.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

networkInterfaces.description: A list of information about network interfaces.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

signalDecoders.description: Information about a list of signals to decode.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

fleets.description: A list of fleet IDs that the vehicle is associated with.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

nodes.description: A list of information about nodes.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

nodes.description: A list of information about nodes.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.

Tags.description: The list of tags assigned to the resource.

vehicles.description: A list of vehicles associated with the fleet.

nextToken.description: The token to retrieve the next set of results, or null if there are no more results.
